questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: Two convex lenses have focal lengths of 50 cm and 25 cm, respectively. If these two lenses are placed in contact, then the net power of this combination will be equal to
questions.solution:
To find the net power of two convex lenses placed in contact, we need to use the formula for the combined power of lenses:

$$ P_{\text{net}} = P_1 + P_2 $$

Here, $ P_1 $ and $ P_2 $ are the powers of the individual lenses. Power (P) is related to the focal length (f) of the lens by the formula:

$$ P = \frac{1}{f} $$

Note that focal length should be in meters to get the power in diopters (D). Let's calculate the power of each lens:

1. For the first lens with a focal length of $ f_1 = 50 $ cm:

$$ P_1 = \frac{1}{f_1} = \frac{1}{0.50} = 2 \, \text{D} $$

2. For the second lens with a focal length of $ f_2 = 25 $ cm:

$$ P_2 = \frac{1}{f_2} = \frac{1}{0.25} = 4 \, \text{D} $$

Adding the powers of the two lenses:

$$ P_{\text{net}} = P_1 + P_2 = 2 \, \text{D} + 4 \, \text{D} = 6 \, \text{D} $$

Therefore, the net power of the combination of these two lenses is +6 diopters.

The correct option is: Option B (+6 dioptre).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText:
Which of the following statements give characteristics of contact forces?

1. It appears between an object when it is in contact with some other object

2. It satisfies the third law of motion

3. It may appear between a pair of solid and fluid

Select the answer using the code given below:
questions.solution:
1. Definition of contact force

    – A force that arises only when two bodies are in physical contact.

  2. Check statement (1): “It appears between an object when it is in contact with some other object”

    – This is exactly the definition of a contact force.

    ⇒ Statement 1 is correct.

  3. Check statement (2): “It satisfies the third law of motion”

    – Newton’s third law, “To every action there is an equal and opposite reaction,” holds for all forces, including contact forces.

    ⇒ Statement 2 is correct.

  4. Check statement (3): “It may appear between a pair of solid and fluid”

    – Examples:

    • Fluid pressure on a submerged plate (normal contact force)

    • Viscous drag between a fluid and a moving solid

    ⇒ Statement 3 is correct.

All three statements are true.

Answer: Option 4 (1, 2 and 3)

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: Which one of the following instruments can be used to measure —250°C temperature ?
options: By using a mercury based thermometer
By using an alcohol based thermometer
questions.solution:
The correct answer is:

$$\text{Option 4: By using thermocouple based thermometer}$$

Step by step:

  1. $-250^\circ \text{C}$ is an extremely low temperature.

  2. A mercury thermometer cannot be used because mercury freezes at about

$$-39^\circ \text{C}$$

  1. An alcohol thermometer can measure lower temperatures than mercury, but not as low as $-250^\circ \text{C}$.

  2. A clinical thermometer is used only for measuring human body temperature, usually in the range

$$35^\circ \text{C} \text{ to } 42^\circ \text{C}$$

  1. A thermocouple thermometer is used for very high as well as very low temperatures.

Therefore, to measure $-250^\circ \text{C}$, we use

$$\boxed{\text{thermocouple based thermometer}}$$

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: A microphone converts
questions.solution:
A microphone is a device that converts sound waves to electrical signals. This process allows the sound waves to be recorded, amplified, or transmitted to other audio devices.

The correct option is:

Option B: sound waves to electrical signals

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: A current of 0.6 A is drawn by an electric bulb for 10 minutes. Which one of the following is the amount of electric charge that flows through the circuit?
questions.solution:
To determine the amount of electric charge that flows through the circuit, we can use the relationship between current, charge, and time. The equation we will use is:

$$Q = I \cdot t$$

where:

  • $ Q $ is the electric charge in coulombs (C).
  • $ I $ is the current in amperes (A).
  • $ t $ is the time in seconds (s).

Given:

  • Current $ I = 0.6 $ A
  • Time $ t = 10 $ minutes

First, we need to convert the time from minutes to seconds:

$$ t = 10 \text{ minutes} \times 60 \text{ seconds/minute} = 600 \text{ seconds} $$

Now we can substitute $ I $ and $ t $ into the equation:

$$ Q = 0.6 \text{ A} \times 600 \text{ s} = 360 \text{ C} $$

Therefore, the amount of electric charge that flows through the circuit is 360 C. Thus, the correct option is:

Option C: 360 C

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: Which one among the following statements is correct ?
options: Pressure has magnitude as well as direction:
As we go deeper in a liquid, the pressure decreases
Action and reaction forces act on the same object
Pascal and N/m<sup>2</sup> represent the same unit
questions.solution:
The correct statement is:

$$\text{Option 4: Pascal and } N/m^2 \text{ represent the same unit}$$

Now let us check each option one by one:

  1. Pressure has magnitude as well as direction

    This is wrong because pressure is a scalar quantity.

    It has only magnitude, not direction.

  2. As we go deeper in a liquid, the pressure decreases

    This is wrong.

    In a liquid, pressure increases with depth.

  3. Action and reaction forces act on the same object

    This is wrong.

    According to Newton’s third law, action and reaction act on different objects.

  4. Pascal and $N/m^2$ represent the same unit

    This is correct because

$$1 \, \text{pascal} = 1 \, N/m^2$$

So, the correct answer is Option 4.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText:
Which of the following pairs of physical phenomenon and the discover is/are correctly matched?

 1. James Chadwick  Photoelectric
 2. Albert Einstein  Neutron
 3. Marie Curie  Radium
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
questions.solution:
The correct matching pair is "Marie Curie and Radium." Marie Curie, along with her husband Pierre Curie, discovered the element Radium. The other pairs are mismatched:

James Chadwick is known for discovering the neutron, not for the photoelectric effect.

Albert Einstein is recognized for his explanation of the photoelectric effect, not for discovering the neutron.

Therefore, the correct answer is Option D: 3 only.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: There is a ball of mass 320 g. It has 625 J potential energy when released freely from a height. The speed with which it will hit the ground is
questions.solution:
First convert the mass to kilograms:

m = 320 g = 0.32 kg

All the potential energy turns into kinetic energy at the bottom:

$$\tfrac12\,m\,v^2 = \text{PE} = 625\text{ J}$$

Solving for v:

$$v = \sqrt{\frac{2\cdot625}{0.32}} = \sqrt{3906.25} = 62.5\text{ m/s}$$

So the ball hits the ground at 62.5 m/s (Option 1).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText:
How many of the following materials can be attracted by a magnet?

1. Plastic

2. Carbon

3. Aluminium

4. Stainless Steel

Select the correct answer using the code given below:
questions.solution:
To determine how many of the listed materials can be attracted by a magnet, we need to understand the magnetic properties of each material.

1. Plastic - Plastic is a non-magnetic material and therefore cannot be attracted by a magnet.

2. Carbon - Pure carbon (in graphite or diamond form) is not magnetic and cannot be attracted by a magnet.

3. Aluminium - Aluminium is a paramagnetic material, meaning it is only weakly attracted to a magnetic field and this attraction is usually negligible. It is generally considered non-magnetic for practical purposes.

4. Stainless Steel - Stainless steel can be ferromagnetic or non-magnetic depending on its composition. Most commonly used stainless steel alloys (like the 300 series) are generally non-magnetic, while some alloys (like the 400 series) can be magnetic. However, since the question does not specify the type of stainless steel, we will consider this ambiguity.

Given the context and the general use cases, we assume the commonly used stainless steel, which is typically non-magnetic.

Based on the above analysis, none of the listed materials are strongly attracted by a magnet.

The correct answer is:

Option D: None

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: A positive charge is moving towards south in a space where magnetic field is pointing in the north direction. The moving charge will experience :
options: a deflecting force towards north direction.
a deflecting force towards east direction.
a deflecting force towards west direction.
questions.solution:
The correct answer is Option D (no deflecting force). Here's why:

Understanding the Concept:

Magnetic Force on a Moving Charge: A charged particle moving in a magnetic field experiences a force only if its velocity has a component perpendicular to the magnetic field direction.

Right-Hand Rule: You can determine the direction of the magnetic force using the right-hand rule. However, in this scenario, we don't need to apply the rule.

Explanation:

Since the positive charge is moving parallel (towards South) to the magnetic field (pointing North), there is no perpendicular component of velocity.

In simpler terms, the charge is moving "along" the magnetic field lines, not "across" them. Therefore, it experiences no magnetic force to deflect it.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: To help deep-sea divers breathe, they carry cylinders of oxygen mixed with

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText:
An iron nail sinks in water whereas an iron ship floats. Which of the following statements is correct in this regard?

1. Average density of ship is greater than that of the water

2. Average density of iron nail is greater than that of the water

3. Average density of the ship is less than that of the water

4. Average density of the ship is equal to that of the water

Select the correct answer using the code given below:
questions.solution:
To understand why an iron nail sinks in water while an iron ship floats, we need to consider the concept of density and buoyancy.

Density is defined as mass per unit volume, and it is given by the formula:

$$ \text{Density} = \frac{\text{Mass}}{\text{Volume}} $$

An iron nail has a high density because its mass is concentrated in a small volume, making its average density greater than that of water. As a result, it sinks.

On the other hand, an iron ship, despite being made of iron, is designed in such a way that it encloses a large volume of air. This design ensures that the overall (or average) density of the ship becomes less than that of water, enabling it to float. The ship's shape and structure cause it to displace a volume of water equal to its own weight before being submerged, allowing it to float due to the buoyant force that is equal to the weight of the displaced water.

Given these points, the correct statements are:

2. The average density of the iron nail is greater than that of water. (Since it sinks)

3. The average density of the ship is less than that of the water. (Since it floats)

Therefore, the correct answer is:

Option B

2 and 3

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: A ball is thrown vertically upward with a speed of 40 m/s. The time taken by the ball to reach the maximum height would be approximately

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: A sound wave has a frequency of 1 kHz and wavelength 50 cm. How long will it take to travel 1 km?

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: Which one of the following is a non-conventional source of energy?
questions.solution:
Non-conventional (renewable or alternative) sources are things like solar, wind, tidal, geothermal, etc.

• Petroleum, natural gas and coal are all conventional (fossil) fuels.

• Tidal energy harnesses the rise and fall of ocean tides, making it a renewable—hence non-conventional—source.

Answer: Option 3 (Tidal energy).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: A ball of 0.1 kg mass is dropped on a hard floor from a height of 0.45 m and rises to a height of 0.2 m. If it was in touch with the floor for 0.1 s, the net force it applied on the floor while bouncing is : (take the gravitational acceleration g = 10 m s⁻²)
questions.solution:
To determine the net force the ball applies on the floor while bouncing, we need to follow these steps:

Step 1: Calculate the velocity of the ball just before it hits the floor

Using the equation of motion:

$$ v^{2} = u^{2} + 2gh $$

where,

u = initial velocity (0 m/s, since the ball is dropped), g = acceleration due to gravity (10 m/s2), h = height from which the ball is dropped (0.45 m).

Therefore,

$$ v^{2} = 0 + 2 \cdot 10 \cdot 0.45 $$

$$ v^{2} = 9 $$

$$ v = \sqrt{9} $$

$$ v = 3 \, \text{m/s} $$

Step 2: Calculate the velocity of the ball just after it bounces off the floor

Using the same equation of motion, but this time with the final height the ball reaches:

$$ v^{2} = u^{2} + 2gh $$

where,

h = height ball rises to (0.2 m).

$$ v^{2} = 0 + 2 \cdot 10 \cdot 0.2 $$

$$ v^{2} = 4 $$

$$ v = \sqrt{4} $$

$$ v = 2 \, \text{m/s} $$

However, this velocity is upward (after the bounce). Thus, we should take it as negative for our momentum calculation to show the direction change.

Step 3: Calculate the change in momentum

Momentum before the bounce:

$$ p_{1} = mv = 0.1 \cdot 3 = 0.3 \, \text{kg m/s} $$

Momentum after the bounce:

$$ p_{2} = m(-v) = 0.1 \cdot (-2) = -0.2 \, \text{kg m/s} $$

Change in momentum:

$$ \Delta p = p_{2} - p_{1} = -0.2 - 0.3 = -0.5 \, \text{kg m/s} $$

Step 4: Calculate the net force

Using Newton's second law:

$$ F = \frac{\Delta p}{\Delta t} $$

where, Δt = time of contact (0.1 s).

Thus,

$$ F = \frac{-0.5}{0.1} = -5 \, \text{N} $$

The magnitude of the force is 5 N. However, we are interested in the net force applied on the floor, which is the positive value of this calculation.

Thus, the net force applied on the floor while bouncing is:

5.0 N

So, the correct answer is Option D: 5.0 N

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: Which one among the following is correct for a person suffering from myopia?
options: The person can see near objects clearly
The person can see distant objects clearly
The person can neither see near objects nor distant objects clearly
questions.solution:
1. Myopia (short-sightedness) means the eye’s optical power is too strong or the eyeball is too long.

  2. Parallel rays from a distant object focus in front of the retina, so distant objects appear blurred.

  3. Rays from a nearby object diverge more and are brought to focus on the retina, so near objects appear clear.

Correct option: 1. The person can see near objects clearly.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: An electric bulb is rated as 220 V and 80 W. When it is operated on 110 V, the power rating would be:
questions.solution:
To determine the power rating of the electric bulb when operated at a different voltage, let's start by using the given ratings. The bulb is rated as 220 V and 80 W. First, we can calculate the resistance of the bulb using the power formula:

$$ P = \frac{V^2}{R} $$

Given:

$$ P = 80 \, \text{W} $$

$$ V = 220 \, \text{V} $$

Rearranging the formula to solve for resistance \( R \):

$$ R = \frac{V^2}{P} $$

Substituting the values:

$$ R = \frac{220^2}{80} $$

Calculating the resistance:

$$ R = \frac{48400}{80} = 605 \, \Omega $$

Now, we will find the power consumed when the bulb is operated at 110 V. Using the power formula again:

$$ P' = \frac{V'^2}{R} $$

Where:

$$ V' = 110 \, \text{V} $$

$$ R = 605 \, \Omega $$

Substituting these values into the formula:

$$ P' = \frac{110^2}{605} $$

Calculating the power:

$$ P' = \frac{12100}{605} \approx 20 \, \text{W} $$

So, when the bulb is operated at 110 V, the power rating would be approximately 20 W. The correct answer is:

Option D: 20 W

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: Which one of the following graphs correctly represents the current (I) - voltage (V) variation for a rectangular piece of a semiconductor wafer?
questions.solution: The current flowing through the semiconductor is not linearly proportional to the voltage. In forward bias the current increases by increasing the voltage in parabolic way.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: If the speed of light in carbon disulfide and vacuum is XXXXX X X and YYYYY Y Y respectively, then
options: X < Y
X > Y
X ≥ Y
X = Y
questions.solution:
Light travels slower in a medium than in vacuum because its refractive index $n>1$. For carbon disulfide ($n_{\rm CS_2}\approx1.63$) we have

speed in CS₂: $X = \dfrac{c}{n_{\rm CS_2}}$

speed in vacuum: $Y = c$

Since $n_{\rm CS_2}>1$,

$$X = \frac{c}{n_{\rm CS_2}} < c = Y\,. $$

Answer: Option 1, $X < Y$.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: Twinkling of stars is due to
options: reflection of starlight from the oceanic surface
questions.solution:
The phenomenon of twinkling of stars is due to the atmospheric refraction of starlight.

When starlight enters the Earth's atmosphere, it passes through layers of air at different temperatures and densities. As light travels through these varying layers, it is continuously refracted, or bent, in different directions. This refraction causes the light of the star to fluctuate in intensity and position as observed from Earth, leading to the perception of the star "twinkling." This effect is more pronounced for stars near the horizon compared to those directly overhead because the light path through the atmosphere is longer and passes through more layers of varying density.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: A point object is placed at the centre of curvature of a spherical concave mirror. Which one among the fol- lowing would be the correct location of image formed?
options: Between the focal point and the centre of curvature
questions.solution:
1. For a concave mirror of radius of curvature $R$, the focal length is

    $$f=\frac{R}{2}$$

  2. Mirror formula (Cartesian sign convention):

    $$\frac{1}{f}=\frac{1}{v}+\frac{1}{u}$$

    where

    • $u$ = object distance from pole (real object ⇒ $u>0$)

    • $v$ = image distance from pole (real image ⇒ $v>0$)

  3. Here the object is at the centre of curvature, so

    $$u=R$$

  4. Substitute into the mirror formula:

    $$\frac{1}{R/2}=\frac{1}{v}+\frac{1}{R}$$

    $$\frac{2}{R}=\frac{1}{v}+\frac{1}{R}$$

    $$\frac{1}{v}=\frac{2}{R}-\frac{1}{R}=\frac{1}{R}$$

    $$\therefore v=R$$

  5. Hence the image is formed at a distance $R$ from the pole, i.e. at the centre of curvature.

Answer: Option 2 – At the centre of curvature.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: Which one of the following statements is NOT correct?
options: An ammeter is always connected in series in the circuit to measure the current
A Voltmeter is always connected to parallel in a circuit to measure the voltage
A voltmeter has a high resistance and an ammeter has a low resistance
A voltmeter has a low resistance and an ammeter has a high resistance
questions.solution:
The incorrect statement is Option 4.

Explanation:

An ammeter must have a low internal resistance and be connected in series so it doesn’t appreciably change the circuit current.

A voltmeter must have a high internal resistance and be connected in parallel so it doesn’t appreciably draw current.

Option 4 reverses these resistances and is therefore not correct.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: A rectangle ABCD is kept in front of a concave mirror of focal length f with its corners A and B being, respectively, at distances 2f and 3f from the mirror with AB along the principal axis as shown in the figure. It forms an image A'B'C'D' in front of the mirror. What is the ratio of B'C' to A'D'?
questions.solution:
The Mirror Equation:

When rays emanating from a point meet at another point after reflection or refraction, that point is termed the image of the initial point.

The image is termed real if the rays actually converge at that point.

It is virtual if the rays do not actually meet but appear to diverge from that point when extended backward.

The mirror equation is given by: $\frac{1}{f} = \frac{1}{v} + \frac{1}{u}$

Linear magnification (m) is defined as the ratio of the height of the image ($h'$) to the height of the object ($h$): $m = \frac{h'}{h} = -\frac{v}{u}$

Calculation:

Given rectangle ABCD with AD = BC ---- (1)

Corners A and B are at distances 2f and 3f from the mirror, respectively.

For corner A (u = 2f): $ \frac{1}{f} = \frac{1}{v} + \frac{1}{2f} \implies v = 2f $

Magnification: $ m = \frac{h'}{h} = -\frac{v}{u} = -\frac{2f}{2f} = -1 $ This means the size of the image is equal to the size of the object: $ AD = A'D' ---- (2) $

For corner B (u = 3f): $ \frac{1}{f} = \frac{1}{v} + \frac{1}{3f} \implies v = \frac{3}{2}f $

Magnification: $ m = \frac{h'}{h} = -\frac{v}{u} = -\frac{\frac{3}{2}f}{3f} = -\frac{1}{2} $ This means the size of the image is half the size of the object: $ B'C' = \frac{1}{2}BC ---- (3) $

From equations 1, 2, and 3, the ratio of B'C' to A'D' is: $ \frac{B'C'}{A'D'} = \frac{\frac{1}{2}BC}{AD} = \frac{1}{2} $

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: A current through a horizontal power line flows in east to west direction. What will be the direction of magnetic field at a point directly below it when viewed from east end?
options: Clockwise in a plane perpendicular to the wire
Anti-clockwise in a plane perpendicular to the wire
Clockwise in a plane of parallel to the wire
Anti-clockwise in a plane of parallel to the wire
questions.solution:
CONCEPT:

Right-hand thumb rule:

According to this rule, if you imagine holding the linear wire conductor in the grip of your right hand so that your thumb points in the direction of the current, the curvature of your fingers around the conductor will represent the direction of the magnetic field lines.

EXPLANATION:

If the current through a horizontal power line flows from east to west, the direction of the magnetic field at a point directly below it will be from north to south. Therefore, the correct option is anti-clockwise in a plane perpendicular to the wire.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Which one of the following statements is not correct?
options: Most carbon compounds are good conductors of electricity.
Bonding in organic compounds is covalent.
Graphite is used as a lubricant.
Diamond is an allotrope of carbon.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Which one of the following statements about a compound is NOT correct?
options: A compound is a substance composed of two or more elements, chemically combined in a fixed proportion
Properties of a compound are different from its constituent elements
A compound is an impure substance
The constituents of a compound can be separated only by chemical or electrochemical reactions
questions.solution:
The statement that is NOT correct is Option 3:

“A compound is an impure substance.”

• By definition, a compound is a pure substance made up of two or more elements chemically combined in fixed proportions.

• Its properties differ from those of the individual elements.

• You can only separate its constituents by chemical or electrochemical means.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: At which temperature does liquid water show maximum density?
questions.solution:
Liquid water reaches its maximum density at about 4 °C, which is 277 K.

• Answer: Option 2 (277 K)

• Reason: Below 4 °C, the increasing prominence of hydrogen-bond–driven open structures makes water expand, so its density peaks right at 4 °C.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: What is the specific purpose of using potassium hydroxide during the saponification process?
options: To obtain soaps which are hard on the skin
To obtain soaps which are soft on the skin
To make the saponification very economical
questions.solution:
The specific purpose of using potassium hydroxide (KOH) during the saponification process is:

Option B To obtain soaps which are soft on the skin

In saponification, different alkalis can be used, and potassium hydroxide typically produces softer soaps. These soaps dissolve more readily in water and are often considered milder and more moisturizing compared to soaps made with sodium hydroxide (NaOH), which tend to be harder.

So, the correct answer is: Option B: To obtain soaps which are soft on the skin

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: The mass number of argon is 40. Which one of the following statements is correct?
options: The number of protons in argon is 22.
The number of neutrons in argon is 18.
The number of electrons in argon is 18.
The sum of numbers of protons and electrons in argon is 40.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Which one of the following nitrogen oxides may dimerize?
questions.solution:
The oxide that dimerizes is NO₂. Here’s why:

NO₂ is a free radical (it has an unpaired electron).

Two NO₂ molecules can pair up that unpaired electron to form dinitrogen tetroxide, N₂O₄.

The equilibrium is:

$$2\,NO_2 \;\rightleftharpoons\; N_2O_4$$

At lower temperatures the equilibrium shifts toward N₂O₄ (the dimer), while at higher temperatures it shifts back to NO₂.

None of the other oxides listed (N₂O, N₂O₃, N₂O₅) form such dimers under normal conditions.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Soap with water forms :
questions.solution:
When soap is mixed with water, it forms a specific type of liquid crystal known as a lyotropic liquid crystal. This occurs because the soap molecules, which are amphiphilic (having both hydrophilic and hydrophobic parts), align themselves in water to form various ordered structures like micelles and liquid crystalline phases. The formation and ordering of these structures depend on the concentration of the soap in water and the temperature.

Lyotropic liquid crystals are distinguished by their dependence on the concentration of the solute (in this case, soap) in a solvent (water), unlike thermotropic liquid crystals, which depend primarily on temperature.

Therefore, the correct answer is :

Option D : Lyotropic liquid crystal

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Litmus, a well-known acid-base indicator, is derived from :
questions.solution:
Litmus is a well-known acid-base indicator and is derived from lichens. Lichens are a symbiotic association between a fungus and an alga or cyanobacterium. When lichens are processed, they yield a mixture of dyes which is used to make litmus paper. The primary lichens used for the production of litmus include species from the genera Rocella and Lecanora.

Thus, the correct answer is :

Option B

Lichens

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: What is the action of litmus on ethanol?
options: Ethanol turns blue litmus to red; confirming acidic nature of ethanol
Ethanol turns red litmus to blue; confirming basic nature of ethanol
questions.solution:
Litmus is a commonly used indicator that helps in determining whether a substance is acidic or basic. Ethanol (C2H5OH) is an alcohol and is generally considered to be a neutral substance. It does not display acidic or basic properties like acids or bases do. Additionally, ethanol does not have a bleaching action.

When litmus paper, whether blue or red, is introduced to ethanol, there is no color change. This indicates that ethanol is neutral towards litmus paper. Therefore, the action of litmus on ethanol can be described by the following option:

Option A: Litmus is neutral towards ethanol.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Which among the following are essential constituents of Portland cement?
questions.solution:
Portland cement is a key material in construction and its essential ingredients must contribute to its properties such as strength, setting time, and durability. Let's break down the components of each option to determine the correct answer:

Option A: Sand, lime, clay

While sand, lime, and clay are important materials in construction, not all of these are essential constituents of Portland cement. Sand is an aggregate used in concrete, but it is not a key ingredient in the cement itself.

Option B: Silica, alumina, lime

This option lists silica, alumina, and lime. These are indeed the principal constituents of Portland cement:

  • Silica ($$SiO_2$$): Provides strength to cement.
  • Alumina ($$Al_2O_3$$): Imparts quick setting ability to the cement.
  • Lime ($$CaO$$): Primary ingredient that reacts with water to form calcium silicate hydrates, giving cement its strength.

Option C: Silica, lime, graphite powder

Graphite powder is not a constituent of Portland cement, which makes this option incorrect.

Option D: Sand, graphene, clay

This option includes sand and graphene, neither of which are required in the chemical composition of Portland cement.

Therefore, the correct answer is:

Option B: Silica, alumina, lime

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Which one of the following is not used as a raw material in the manufacture of glass?
questions.solution:
Gypsum is not used as a raw material in the manufacture of glass.

Glass is typically made from the following raw materials:

Soda (Sodium carbonate, also known as soda ash): Serves as a flux to lower the melting temperature of the sand.

Silica (in the form of sand): The main ingredient, giving glass its basic structure.

Lime (calcium oxide or calcium carbonate): Acts as a stabilizing agent ensuring the durability of the glass.

Alumina: Used to enhance the chemical durability and mechanical strength of the glass.

Borax (Sodium borate): Sometimes used in specialty glasses to improve thermal and chemical resistance.

Gypsum, primarily composed of calcium sulfate, is typically used in construction (as drywall or plaster) rather than in glass production.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Dry ice is used on a performing stage to produce mist in air. The process involved is an example of
questions.solution:
The correct answer is Option A: sublimation. Here's why:

Sublimation is the process where a substance transitions directly from a solid phase to a gaseous phase, bypassing the liquid phase.

Here's how it applies to dry ice:

  • Dry ice is frozen carbon dioxide (CO2).

  • At room temperature and standard atmospheric pressure, CO2 exists as a gas.

  • When dry ice is exposed to air, it absorbs heat energy from the surroundings.

  • This heat energy causes the CO2 molecules in the dry ice to vibrate faster and transition directly from a solid to a gas.

This gaseous CO2 is what creates the mist or fog effect on stage. The mist is not actually water vapor, but tiny droplets of liquid water that condense around the cold CO2 gas.

Let's look at why the other options are incorrect:

  • Evaporation: Involves a transition from liquid to gas (like water boiling). Dry ice skips the liquid phase.

  • Condensation: Is the opposite of evaporation (gas to liquid), like dew forming on grass.

  • Precipitation: Involves water falling from the atmosphere as rain, snow, sleet, or hail.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Which one of the following metals can be extracted using carbon as reducing agent?
questions.solution:
To determine which metal can be extracted using carbon as a reducing agent, we need to consider the reactivity of the metals. Carbon is typically used to reduce the oxides of metals that are less reactive than carbon itself in the reactivity series. Here's a brief explanation for each metal mentioned in the options:

Option A: Zinc

Zinc is generally extracted from its ore using carbon. The chemical equation for the reduction of zinc oxide by carbon can be written as:

$$ \text{ZnO} + \text{C} \rightarrow \text{Zn} + \text{CO} $$

Here, carbon acts as a reducing agent, converting zinc oxide (ZnO) to zinc (Zn).

Option B: Silver

Silver is usually extracted by other methods such as the amalgamation process or cyanide process rather than using carbon. This is because silver is already quite unreactive and exists in nature mostly in its elemental form or in ores that do not require reduction by carbon.

Option C: Gold

Gold is even more unreactive than silver and is usually found in its native state. Therefore, it is not extracted using carbon. Instead, processes like cyanidation are employed to extract gold from its ores.

Option D: Aluminum

Aluminum is a highly reactive metal and cannot be extracted by reduction with carbon. Instead, it is extracted using electrolysis of aluminum oxide in the Hall-Héroult process.

Based on this analysis, the metal that can be extracted using carbon as a reducing agent is:

Option A: Zinc

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Which one among the following compounds has a sweet and fruity smell?
questions.solution:
Among the given compounds, the one that has a sweet and fruity smell is Ethyl acetate.

Option A: Ethyl alcohol (ethanol) has a characteristic smell often associated with alcoholic beverages and is not typically described as fruity.

Option B: Acetic acid has a strong, pungent vinegar-like smell.

Option C: Ethyl acetate is known for its sweet, fruity aroma and is commonly used in perfumes, as well as in flavorings and as a solvent. Its molecular formula is $$\text{CH}_3\text{COOCH}_2\text{CH}_3$$ and it is an ester, which are often known for their pleasant, fruity smells.

Option D: Acetophenone has a sweet but more intense smell, often described as reminiscent of almonds or cherries, but not typically purely fruity.

Therefore, the correct answer is:

Option C: Ethyl acetate

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Element X forms a chloride with the formula XCl₂, which is a solid with high melting point. X would most likely be in the same group of periodic table as
questions.solution:
XCl₂ being a high-melting solid tells us it’s an ionic compound with X in the +2 oxidation state. Metals that commonly form MCl₂ salts are the Group 2 (alkaline-earth) elements.

• Group 2 elements → Be, Mg, Ca, …

• From the options given, Mg is the only Group 2 metal.

Answer: Option 3 (Mg)

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Vapours of sulphur escaping from a volcano often form a crust on the rocks. The process involved is an example of
questions.solution:
When vapors of sulfur escape from a volcano and form a crust on the rocks, the process involved is known as deposition. Deposition is the phase transition in which a substance goes directly from a gas to a solid without passing through the liquid phase. This is observed when sulfur vapors cool rapidly and solidify on the surfaces of rocks.

Therefore, the correct answer is:

Option C: deposition

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Which one of the following is the correct arrangement of metals in the decreasing order of their reactivity?
questions.solution:
The correct arrangement of metals in the decreasing order of their reactivity is:

Option D: Sodium, Iron, Copper, Silver

Here's the reasoning:

Sodium (Na) is highly reactive and is known to react vigorously with water.

Iron (Fe) is also quite reactive, especially when it reacts with oxygen and water to form rust.

Copper (Cu) is less reactive compared to sodium and iron but can still react with certain substances.

Silver (Ag) is one of the least reactive metals and does not tarnish or corrode easily.

Thus, the correct order from the most reactive to the least reactive is: $ \text{Sodium (Na)} > \text{Iron (Fe)} > \text{Copper (Cu)} > \text{Silver (Ag)} $

This matches Option D.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Equal volume of all gases, when measured at the same temperature and pressure, contain an equal number of particles. Who proposed the above law?
questions.solution:
The correct answer is Option C: Avogadro.

Avogadro's Law states that equal volumes of all gases, at the same temperature and pressure, contain the same number of molecules.

Here's a breakdown of why the other options are incorrect:

Charles: Charles's Law describes the relationship between the volume and temperature of a gas at constant pressure.

Boyle: Boyle's Law describes the relationship between the volume and pressure of a gas at constant temperature.

Lussac: Gay-Lussac's Law describes the relationship between the pressure and temperature of a gas at constant volume.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Bronze is an alloy of
questions.solution:
The correct answer is Option B: Cu and Sn.

Here's why:

Bronze is a metal alloy primarily composed of copper (Cu) and tin (Sn). It's known for its durability, strength, and resistance to corrosion.

Let's look at the other options:

  • Option A: Cu and Zn - This describes brass, not bronze.
  • Option C: Zn and Mg - This is not a common metal alloy.
  • Option D: Fe and Cu - This combination is not typically used to make bronze.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Which one of the following reactions is an example of decomposition reaction?
options: CH4 (g) + 2O2 (g) → CO2(g) + 2H2O(g)

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: <!-- x-tinymce/html -->Which one among the following is an example of chemical change?
questions.solution:
Mixing NaOH and HCl is a chemical change. In that reaction new substances form (salt and water):

$$\text{NaOH} + \text{HCl} \;\longrightarrow\; \text{NaCl} + \text{H}_2\text{O}$$

Quick check of the other options:

• Sublimation of camphor – physical (phase change)

• Melting of ice – physical (phase change)

• Heating an iron rod – physical (thermal expansion/colour change)

So the correct answer is Option 4.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: For manufacturing of glass, which among the following is used as a source of silica?
questions.solution:
For the manufacturing of glass, the source of silica used is:

Option D: Sand

Sand is primarily composed of silicon dioxide (SiO₂), which is the main ingredient in glass production. The silicon dioxide from sand melts at high temperatures and then cools to form glass.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Which among the following is the popular method for manufacture of ammonia?
questions.solution:
The popular method for the manufacture of ammonia is:

Option B: Haber-Bosch process

The Haber-Bosch process is widely used industrially to synthesize ammonia ($$\text{NH}_3$$) from nitrogen ($$\text{N}_2$$) and hydrogen ($$\text{H}_2$$) gases. Here is a brief description:

Reaction: $$ \text{N}_2(g) + 3\text{H}_2(g) \rightarrow 2\text{NH}_3(g) $$

Conditions: Typically conducted at high pressures (150-200 atm) and high temperatures (400-500 °C) with an iron catalyst.

This process was developed by Fritz Haber and Carl Bosch, and it revolutionized agriculture by providing a method to produce large quantities of ammonia, which is a key ingredient in fertilizers.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Which of the following anions has a valency of -3 ?
questions.solution:
1. Valency of an anion = magnitude of its charge.

  2. Write the formula and charge for each:

    a. Nitride: $N^{3-}$ ⇒ valency = 3 (charge = –3)

    b. Nitrate: $NO_{3}^{-}$ ⇒ valency = 1 (charge = –1)

    c. Sulphide: $S^{2-}$ ⇒ valency = 2 (charge = –2)

    d. Sulphate: $SO_{4}^{2-}$ ⇒ valency = 2 (charge = –2)

  3. The only anion with valency 3 (charge –3) is nitride.

Answer: Option 1 (Nitride).

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText:
Match List I with List II and select the correct answer using the code given below the Lists:

LIST I

(Process)

LIST II

(Separation method)

A.

Separation of acetone and water from their mixture

1.

Chromatography

B.

Separation of water
and kerosene oil
from their mixture

2.

Centrifugation

C.

Separation of cream from milk

3.

Distillation

D.

Separation of pigments from plant extract

4.

Separating Funnel
questions.solution:
Here's a breakdown of the separation methods and why they match with the given processes:

A. Separation of acetone and water from their mixture:

Correct Answer: 3. Distillation

Acetone and water are miscible liquids (they dissolve in each other). Distillation takes advantage of their different boiling points. Acetone has a lower boiling point than water, so it will evaporate first when the mixture is heated. The vapor is then collected and condensed back into a liquid, separating it from the water.

B. Separation of water and kerosene oil from their mixture:

Correct Answer: 4. Separating Funnel

Water and kerosene oil are immiscible liquids (they don't mix). A separating funnel allows for the separation based on density. Kerosene is less dense than water, so it will float on top. The heavier water is drained out from the bottom of the funnel, leaving the kerosene oil behind.

C. Separation of cream from milk:

Correct Answer: 2. Centrifugation

Centrifugation uses centrifugal force to separate components with different densities. Cream is less dense than the rest of the milk. When spun rapidly, the heavier milk settles at the bottom, while the lighter cream rises to the top, allowing for easy separation.

D. Separation of pigments from plant extract:

Correct Answer: 1. Chromatography

Chromatography is used to separate different components of a mixture based on their affinity for a stationary phase and a mobile phase. Plant extracts often contain multiple pigments with different properties. Chromatography allows for the separation of these pigments by their varying rates of movement through the stationary phase.

Therefore, the correct answer is Option C: A - 3, B - 4, C - 2, D - 1.
